free_text: Same as J.Chem.Phys. 46,3273(1967)
On the low-frequency motions in solid benzene by
inelastic scattering of cold neutrons
Cold neutron facility consisting of a
beryllium filter and a chopper with bent slits
reactor
The calculated mean energy of incoming neutrons
was 4.18 milli-eV
Solid benzene, 0.4 mm thickness,  cooled by a flow of
cold gas boiled off from liquid nitrogen.
Measurement of the scattered-neutron spectra for a
scattering angle of 45 deg. and six temperatures
neutrons
The arbitrary neutron intensity scales for the several
temperatures are normalized with respect to one another
except the 77 deg. K scale.
Data from privcom Bally 1968
conversion of Dastar 656,657,699-702
